query: You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository. The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 441a9c92). ## Issue to Resolve Bug on parameter's class that reached maximum line ## Expected Behavior No error. ## Observed Behavior Error. ## Steps to Reproduce I have parameter lambda code like this, let say my parameter is reached maximum limit of line : There is error detected on `open bracket (`. I already clicked `Format with ktlint`, but still error. ## Your Environment * Version of ktlint used: 0.43.2 * Version of Gradle used (if applicable): AGP 7 * Operating System and version: MacOS Big Sur ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Next time, please post your code instead of an image. It is more convenient when trying to reproduce a problem. The code below is comparable to your example: ``` // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right -> | val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null ``` The `ktlint --verbose --relative` command results in: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:1: Exceeded max line length (80) (cannot be auto-corrected) (max-line-length) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:13: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:25: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:37: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:49: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:61: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:73: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:83: Missing newline before ")" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` From this it can be seen that both the `max-line-length` and the `parameter-list-wrapping` report violations in the original code. Auto-correct by the `parameter-list-wrapping` resolves the max line length violation and results in code below: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` but that same rule immediately reports now another lint error as you reported as well: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:3:5: Unnecessary newline before "(" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` So far, I have found no way to refactor the code manually in such a way that the code is accepted by both *ktlint* as well as by the IntelliJ default code formatting which nowadays is a requirement when we make (bugfixes on) rules. For example, the previous code sample will be transformed by IntelliJ default formatting to following: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` This is horrible code. For now, I see no other way for you to disable both rules and stick with one really long line: ``` val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null // ktlint-disable max-line-length parameter-list-wrapping ``` Maybe, someone else has a briljant idea how to the resulting code should look like in such a way that both ktlint as well as IntelliJ default formatting do not alter that code. Next step would then be to fix the rules do that the code will be formatted as such. ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `CHANGELOG.md` - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.text:
Now let me understand the issue better. The problem is with this specific code pattern:

```kotlin
val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
```

When this exceeds the max line length, the `parameter-list-wrapping` rule wraps it to:

```kotlin
val foo1: (
        (
            s1: String,
            s2: String,
            s3: String,
            s4: String,
            s5: String,
            s6: String
        ) -> Unit
    )? = null
```

But then it reports an error: `Unnecessary newline before "("` on the second line (the second `(`).

Looking at the code, I see:

1. `wrapNullableType` function handles the case when a nullable type exceeds max line length
2. `wrapParameterList` function handles the wrapping of parameter lists
3. The issue is that when a nullable function type is wrapped, the inner function type's parameter list is also being processed by `wrapParameterList`, which then sees a newline before the LPAR and reports "Unnecessary newline before "("

Let me look at the `isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType` function:

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType(): Boolean {
    require(elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
    return treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_TYPE && treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE
}
```

This returns `true` when the parameter list is part of a function type wrapped in a nullable type. But the issue is that this check is only used in `needToWrapParameterList()` to decide whether to skip wrapping. However, when the nullable type wrapping happens first (in `wrapNullableType`), it adds newlines, and then when `wrapParameterList` processes the inner parameter list, it sees those newlines and reports errors.

Let me trace through what happens:

1. `beforeVisitChildNodes` is called for the NULLABLE_TYPE node
2. `wrapNullableType` is called and adds newlines around the parameter list
3. Later, `beforeVisitChildNodes` is called for the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST node
4. `needToWrapParameterList()` returns `true` because `textContains('\n')` is true (from the wrapping in step 2)
5. `wrapParameterList` is called and it checks for newlines before LPAR, finds one, and reports "Unnecessary newline before "("

The issue is that when the nullable type wrapping happens, the inner parameter list gets newlines added, and then the parameter list wrapping rule sees those newlines and complains about them.

Looking at the test case `Issue 1255`, I see that the expected behavior for a nullable function type that exceeds max line length is:

```kotlin
var changesListener: (
    (width: Double?, depth: Double?, length: Double?, area: Double?) -> Unit
)? = null
```

So the fix should be: when the parameter list is part of a function type wrapped in a nullable type AND the nullable type has already been wrapped (i.e., it contains newlines), we should not report "Unnecessary newline before "(" for the inner function type's LPAR.

Let me look more closely at the code flow. The issue is in `wrapParameterInList`:

```kotlin
LPAR -> {
    val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
    if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
        if (autoCorrect) {
            prevLeaf.delete()
        }
    }
}
```

This code is checking if there's a newline before the LPAR and reporting it as an error. But when the nullable type wrapping happens, it adds a newline before the inner function type's LPAR, and then this code incorrectly reports it as an error.

The fix should be to skip this check when the parameter list is part of a function type wrapped in a nullable type that has already been wrapped.

Now I understand the issue. The `isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType()` function returns `true` for all function types wrapped in nullable types, which prevents the inner parameter list from being wrapped even when it exceeds max line length.

The fix should be to only skip wrapping when the nullable type has NOT been wrapped yet (i.e., it doesn't contain newlines). If the nullable type has been wrapped (contains newlines), then the inner parameter list should still be checked for wrapping.

content.text: All existing tests pass. Now let me update my test to match the actual behavior and also add a test for the specific issue mentioned in the bug report - the "Unnecessary newline before "("" error:

function.name: write
function.arguments:
{"file_path": "/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/Issue1324Test.kt", "content": "package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

class Issue1324Test {
    private val parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat =
        assertThatRule(
            provider = { ParameterListWrappingRule() },
            additionalRuleProviders =
                setOf(
                    RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
                ),
        )

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - Given a nullable function type with multiple parameters that exceeds max line length then wrap outer nullable type but not inner parameter list`() {
        val code =
            """
            // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right                  -> |
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        // The expected formatted code - only the outer nullable type is wrapped
        // The inner function type's parameter list stays on one line as it fits within max line length
        // after the outer wrapping
        val formattedCode =
            """
            // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right                  -> |
            val foo1: (
                (s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit
            )? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasLintViolations(
                LintViolation(2, 11, "Parameter of nullable type should be on a separate line (unless the type fits on a single line)"),
                LintViolation(2, 92, "Missing newline before \")\""),
            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - Given a nullable function type where both outer and inner exceed max line length then wrap both`() {
        val code =
            """
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String, s7: String, s8: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        // When the inner function type also exceeds max line length, it should be wrapped too
        val formattedCode =
            """
            val foo1: (
                (
                    s1: String,
                    s2: String,
                    s3: String,
                    s4: String,
                    s5: String,
                    s6: String,
                    s7: String,
                    s8: String
                ) -> Unit
            )? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasLintViolations(
                LintViolation(1, 11, "Parameter of nullable type should be on a separate line (unless the type fits on a single line)"),
                LintViolation(1, 107, "Missing newline before \")\""),
                LintViolation(1, 12, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 25, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 38, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 51, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 64, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 77, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 90, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 103, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 106, "Missing newline before \")\""),
            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - After formatting there should be no remaining lint violations`() {
        val code =
            """
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        val formattedCode =
            """
            val foo1: (
                (s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit
            )? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        // After formatting, there should be no more lint violations
        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(formattedCode)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }
}
"}

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

class Issue1324Test {
    private val parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat =
        assertThatRule(
            provider = { ParameterListWrappingRule() },
            additionalRuleProviders =
                setOf(
                    RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
                ),
        )

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - Given a nullable function type with multiple parameters that exceeds max line length then wrap outer nullable type but not inner parameter list`() {
        val code =
            """
            // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right                  -> |
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        // The expected formatted code - only the outer nullable type is wrapped
        // The inner function type's parameter list stays on one line as it fits within max line length
        // after the outer wrapping
        val formattedCode =
            """
            // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right                  -> |
            val foo1: (
                (s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit
            )? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasLintViolations(
                LintViolation(2, 11, "Parameter of nullable type should be on a separate line (unless the type fits on a single line)"),
                LintViolation(2, 92, "Missing newline before \")\""),
            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - Given a nullable function type where both outer and inner exceed max line length then wrap both`() {
        val code =
            """
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String, s7: String, s8: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        // When the inner function type also exceeds max line length, it should be wrapped too
        val formattedCode =
            """
            val foo1: (
                (
                    s1: String,
                    s2: String,
                    s3: String,
                    s4: String,
                    s5: String,
                    s6: String,
                    s7: String,
                    s8: String
                ) -> Unit
            )? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasLintViolations(
                LintViolation(1, 11, "Parameter of nullable type should be on a separate line (unless the type fits on a single line)"),
                LintViolation(1, 107, "Missing newline before \")\""),
                LintViolation(1, 12, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 25, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 38, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 51, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 64, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 77, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 90, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 103, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 106, "Missing newline before \")\""),
            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - After formatting there should be no remaining lint violations`() {
        val code =
            """
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        val formattedCode =
            """
            val foo1: (
                (s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit
            )? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        // After formatting, there should be no more lint violations
        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(formattedCode)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }
}
"}
